description: LeftBehind triage benchmark — post-procedure patient-reported contacts, labeled with ground-truth disposition/urgency/red_flag; the inbound stream the product triages

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and I'm burping up a lot of gas since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating from air insufflation are expected and typically resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Classic benign post-colonoscopy symptoms with no red flags; self-care only.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor said you took out a small polyp. When will I find out what it was, and do I need another colonoscopy sooner now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and future surveillance interval
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: a single 5 mm polyp was removed and pathology is pending (expected ~2026-08-09); the team will call with the result and set the surveillance interval, likely 7-10 years if 1-2 small tubular adenomas.
contacts.rationale: Directly answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item F1 and the surveillance-interval item F2.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a tiny bit of pink when I wiped after a bowel movement today. No pain, and it stopped. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of a small polyp; no ongoing bleeding, no clot, no hemodynamic symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; minor self-limited spotting can occur after a small cold-snare polypectomy. Advise return precautions: call or seek care for repeated passage of dark/red blood, clots, lightheadedness, or severe abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign trap: a small amount of pink after a clean cold-snare polypectomy in a patient on no anticoagulants is expected; the correct disposition is reassurance with clear return precautions rather than escalation.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and I've been really gassy and bloated since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatulence
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the polyps you took out, and is it okay to start my ibuprofen again for my knee?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology result and safe timing to resume the held OTC NSAID
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the record: pathology expected back ~2026-08-04 and the team will call with the result and surveillance interval; ibuprofen may resume after 5-7 days if there has been no rectal bleeding; aspirin and fish oil were never held.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the NSAID reconciliation/resumption item (F3) and the pending pathology (F1).

contacts.verbatim: I restarted my ibuprofen a couple days ago and now I've passed a bunch of dark red blood in the toilet twice today and I feel kind of dizzy and weak.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage, likely aggravated by early NSAID resumption, with a hemodynamic symptom (lightheadedness)
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day evaluation / ED referral; instruct to stop ibuprofen immediately; assess vitals and hemoglobin; anticipate possible repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis. Counsel that NSAID was resumed earlier than advised.
contacts.rationale: Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding with lightheadedness is a red-flag complication requiring urgent action; the premature NSAID resumption is a triage trap that compounds bleeding risk.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm real gassy and crampy in my belly. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat plus insufflation-related bloating and mild cramping after a routine surveillance colonoscopy
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat, gas, and mild cramping are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Walking and passing gas help. Return precautions for heavy bleeding, severe pain, or fever.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms after air insufflation; no red flag, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: I held my diabetes pill the day before and the day of the test like you said — am I okay to go back to taking it now that I'm eating normally?
contacts.clinical_signal: Diabetic patient asking about resuming metformin that was held only for prep/procedure day
contacts.expected_action: Confirm from the plan: metformin was held only for the prep/procedure day; resume metformin 1000 mg BID now that she is eating a normal diet. Reassure no bridging or other med changes needed.
contacts.rationale: Directly answerable from the med-reconciliation follow-up item; benign continuation-of-care question, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the polyps you took out, and do I need another scope sooner because of my dad's colon cancer?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology turnaround and how family history affects her surveillance interval
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-11); the team will call with the result and set the surveillance interval based on histology and her single first-degree relative diagnosed after age 60. No action needed by the patient meanwhile.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from pending_pathology and surveillance_interval items; benign informational question, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a little scratchy and I'm pretty gassy and bloated since yesterday. Otherwise fine.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation bloating; no bleeding, no pain
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected and self-limited within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms after a low-risk cold-snare polypectomy; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out about the polyp they took out? And is it okay that I've gone back to my fish oil?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and OTC fish oil (mistaken 'blood thinner') resumption
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-11), GI team will call with result and set surveillance interval. Reassure that OTC fish oil is a supplement, was never a required hold, and is fine to continue.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending_pathology (F1). No anticoagulant was held, so nothing to time.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and nobody ever called me about the biopsy from my colonoscopy. I'm starting to worry something got missed.
contacts.clinical_signal: Dropped follow-up — pathology result not communicated to patient within expected window
contacts.expected_action: Retrieve specimen A pathology (expected back ~2026-08-11), have GI call the patient with the result, document the surveillance interval (F2), and close the loop; flag the missed result-communication step.
contacts.rationale: Classic dropped-ball: a pending_pathology item (F1) whose result was never relayed. Not a clinical emergency, but requires active scheduling/outreach to close the follow-up loop.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm burping a lot and feel gassy since yesterday.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat plus insufflation-related bloating/gas after bidirectional exam
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating/gas are expected after EGD and colonoscopy and typically settle within 24-48h. Advise fluids and walking; return precautions given.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out about my biopsies? And do I go back on my baby aspirin and my ibuprofen for my knee now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and whether to restart self-started aspirin / NSAIDs
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies pending (~day 4-5), team will call with results. Advise to keep NSAIDs/ibuprofen stopped indefinitely and to NOT restart aspirin yet — the ulcer is a likely bleeding source; antiplatelet plan will be decided with PCP/cardiology.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the med_reconciliation item (do-not-resume/avoid guidance). Counseling, not escalation.

contacts.verbatim: I'm feeling much better, cramping is gone. When should I get my blood counts rechecked to see if the iron is working?
contacts.clinical_signal: Recovering patient asking about anemia response lab monitoring timing
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: recheck CBC/ferritin in ~6-8 weeks after iron repletion and source control; continue iron with vitamin C, separated from tea/coffee; keep taking omeprazole.
contacts.rationale: Routine informational question answerable from the follow-up plan; links to the lab_followup item. No escalation.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a little streak of pink when I wiped this morning and my throat's scratchy. Should I be worried?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor spotting expected after forceps biopsy of a friable mass plus post-intubation sore throat; no hemodynamic symptoms, single small amount
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of spotting after biopsy of a friable lesion and a scratchy throat are expected and self-limited. Review return precautions for heavy/repeated bleeding, lightheadedness, or inability to pass stool/gas.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: minimal spotting after a diagnostic biopsy is expected; no red flag without volume, recurrence, or hemodynamic change.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear about the biopsy from the mass they found? And should I keep taking my baby aspirin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology of the suspicious mass and about continuing self-started low-dose aspirin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected back ~2026-07-29; team will call with the result and coordinate oncology/surgical workup. Reconcile aspirin 81 mg — no need to stop now for the diagnostic scope; a peri-operative hold/resume plan will be given before any surgery.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending_pathology (F1) and med_reconciliation (F6).

contacts.verbatim: I haven't been able to pass stool or gas since yesterday, my belly is swollen and cramping bad, and I threw up twice.
contacts.clinical_signal: Obstipation, abdominal distension, cramping, and vomiting — progression of the known partially obstructing sigmoid mass to complete large-bowel obstruction
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day emergency evaluation / ED; obtain abdominal imaging, keep NPO, and involve surgery urgently for possible complete large-bowel obstruction.
contacts.rationale: Obstipation with distension and vomiting in a patient with a known partially obstructing sigmoid mass is a true, emergent complication requiring immediate escalation.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is scratchy and I've been really gassy and bloated since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When should I expect to hear about the biopsies from my polyps? And I want to make sure my bad-nausea-from-anesthesia note actually got written down this time.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and confirming PONV is documented for future sedation
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: histology pending, expected back ~2026-08-11; team will call with result and the family-history-adjusted surveillance interval. Confirm PONV (not motion sickness) is recorded for future anesthesia.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item and the PONV record-correction item.

contacts.verbatim: I'm a bit scared — I saw a little streak of blood on the toilet paper after a bowel movement this morning. No pain, and it hasn't happened again.
contacts.clinical_signal: TRIAGE TRAP (dangerous-sounding, benign): minor self-limited spotting after cold-snare removal of two diminutive polyps; no clots, no recurrent bleeding, no lightheadedness or abdominal pain
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of spotting after diminutive cold-snare polypectomy is expected and self-limited. Give clear return precautions: call/seek care for large-volume bleeding, passing clots, lightheadedness/dizziness, or severe abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Sounds alarming but is a normal minor post-polypectomy finding for tiny cold-snare sites; the trap is over-escalating. Only true red flags (heavy/repeated bleeding, hemodynamic symptoms, severe pain) warrant escalation.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and nobody has called me about my biopsy results. I'm anxious not knowing, especially with my dad's colon cancer.
contacts.clinical_signal: Pathology result not yet communicated to patient — potential dropped follow-up on the pending_pathology loop
contacts.expected_action: Locate the histology result (expected ~2026-08-11), have GI call the patient with the result, and set/document the family-history-adjusted surveillance interval; correct the average-risk classification on record.
contacts.rationale: Flags a follow-up that may have been dropped; links to the pending_pathology item and the surveillance-interval correction. Disposition is to close the loop, not clinically emergent.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a little scratchy and I'm real bloated and burping up gas since yesterday.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-procedure sore throat plus insufflation bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy with air insufflation and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor said they found something and took a sample. When will I know what it is? And can I take my ibuprofen again, my back is acting up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending biopsy result and whether to restart held OTC ibuprofen
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: sigmoid biopsy pathology pending (expected ~2026-07-28); GI will personally call with the result and next steps. Advise ibuprofen stays HELD given the GI bleeding and pending workup; use acetaminophen for back pain instead and do not restart NSAID without GI sign-off.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item and the ibuprofen do-not-resume reconciliation (F6). No red flag.

contacts.verbatim: Saw a tiny bit of pink on the toilet paper once today, no real blood. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor self-limited spotting after cold snare polypectomy / biopsy; no active bleeding, no hemodynamic symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of pink spotting on the tissue after cold snare polypectomy and biopsy is expected and self-limited. Give return precautions: call or seek care for heavy or repeated red blood, clots, lightheadedness, or severe abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: trivial one-time spotting after low-risk cold snare polypectomy is expected, not delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage; reassure with clear escalation triggers.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I've been really gassy and bloated since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-procedure sore throat + insufflation bloating after colonoscopy
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild throat irritation and gas/bloating from insufflation are expected and typically resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor said he took out two little polyps. When will I find out what they were, and do I need another colonoscopy? I'm 76 so I wasn't sure if I'd need one again.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and the surveillance interval
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-05); team will call with the result and then set an individualized surveillance interval that accounts for her age and health status.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item and the surveillance_interval item.

contacts.verbatim: I passed a big amount of bright red blood into the toilet twice this afternoon, with some clots, and now I feel dizzy and a bit sweaty when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage with orthostatic/hemodynamic symptoms; ascending-colon cold-snare site within the typical delayed-bleed window
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day evaluation / send to ED; assess vitals and hemoglobin, IV access, type and screen; anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis of the polypectomy site.
contacts.rationale: Substantial hematochezia with clots plus a hemodynamic symptom days after polypectomy is a red-flag delayed post-polypectomy bleed requiring urgent action; no anticoagulant was held, so this is a spontaneous post-polypectomy bleed.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and my belly is full of gas since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: You took a little piece out during the test — when will I know what it was, and will someone call me? I don't use that online message thing.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending polyp pathology and reiterating phone-only contact preference
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: single 4 mm ascending polyp removed, pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-08); GI will call by phone with the result and next screening interval. Confirm phone-only preference is flagged.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and reinforces the phone-contact care-coordination flag.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a tiny bit of pink when I wiped once today. No pain, no real bleeding, everything else feels fine. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace self-limited spotting after cold-snare polypectomy in a patient on no anticoagulation; no hemodynamic or volume red flags
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a trace of pink after a small cold-snare polypectomy is expected and self-limited. Give return precautions: call or seek care for repeated red blood, clots, black stools, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap — dangerous-sounding minor spotting that is actually benign after a 4 mm cold-snare polypectomy with no anticoagulant and no hemodynamic symptoms; reassure with clear return precautions rather than escalate.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm all bloated and windy since yesterday.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild throat irritation and gas/bloating are expected and typically resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The nurse said I could start my warfarin again the night of the test, and I did. When will I hear back about the polyps, and is it okay that I'm back on the blood thinner already?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and confirming warfarin resumption timing; resumption was per plan (evening of procedure), no bleeding reported
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-16), GI will call with results and surveillance interval; confirm warfarin resumption the evening of the procedure was correct per plan and remind her the anticoagulation clinic will recheck INR within 5-7 days. Review bleeding precautions.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; resumption matches the documented med_resume plan, so this is reassurance/information rather than a med-timing error. Links to pending_pathology (F1).

contacts.verbatim: I saw a little streak of pink on the toilet paper once this morning. No dizziness, no real bleeding, everything else feels normal.
contacts.clinical_signal: Single episode of minor spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of small polyps; no recurrent bleeding, no clots, no hemodynamic symptoms, hemostasis confirmed intraprocedure
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scant one-time spotting after small cold-snare polypectomy is common and self-limited. Advise return precautions: seek care for repeated or large-volume red blood, clots, black stools, lightheadedness, or fast/irregular heartbeat, especially while re-anticoagulated.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap — dangerous-sounding (bleeding on anticoagulation) but benign: a single trace of pink after a clean cold-snare exam with no volume, recurrence, or hemodynamic signal does not meet escalation criteria. Return precautions are reinforced against the F3 delayed-bleeding watch.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a bit scratchy and I'm pretty gassy and bloated since yesterday. Otherwise okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating; no red flags
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and self-resolve within 24-48h; encourage walking and hydration.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no bleeding or pain; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor took out a few polyps. When will I know what they were, and is it okay that I kept taking my baby aspirin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending polyp pathology and about continued low-dose aspirin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected ~2026-08-03, team will call with results and the surveillance interval; confirm the 81 mg aspirin was intentionally continued, is now documented, and no change is needed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to pending_pathology (F1) and reinforces the med reconciliation (F3).

contacts.verbatim: I just passed a bunch of dark red blood in the toilet — twice in the last hour — and I'm feeling shaky and a little dizzy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage with orthostatic symptoms; bleeding risk heightened by uninterrupted low-dose aspirin and the 9 mm hot-snare ascending resection
contacts.expected_action: Escalate immediately for same-day ED evaluation; assess hemodynamics/CBC; anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis at the ascending polypectomy site; review antiplatelet status.
contacts.rationale: Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding with a hemodynamic symptom (lightheadedness) is a genuine red-flag complication requiring urgent evaluation; the continued aspirin (F3) is the relevant bleeding-risk factor.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and I've been really burpy and bloated since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat plus insufflation-related bloating/belching
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and air-related bloating are expected after upper endoscopy and self-resolve within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms after a diagnostic EGD with biopsies only; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out what the biopsies showed? And did they see anything bad in my esophagus?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending esophageal/gastric pathology and results communication
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: EGD showed treatable acid-reflux inflammation (LA Grade B esophagitis) and no visible Barrett's or mass; biopsies pending (expected ~2026-08-11), team will call with results and any surveillance plan.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure report and follow-up plan; maps to the pending esophageal pathology item.

contacts.verbatim: I've got one of my headaches coming on — am I okay to take my ibuprofen, or should I be avoiding it now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient clarifying NSAID use given its role in her erosive esophagitis; medication-optimization counseling opportunity
contacts.expected_action: Counsel to avoid/minimize ibuprofen because it contributed to the esophageal inflammation; use acetaminophen for headaches and follow up with PCP on headache pattern; reinforce pre-meal BID PPI dosing.
contacts.rationale: Benign, answerable medication question that maps to the medication-reconciliation/optimization follow-up item; no held anticoagulant is involved, so no bleeding-risk escalation.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little raw and my chest feels kind of sore where the food used to stick. No throwing up, no fever.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat plus mild retrosternal soreness after the scope traversed the narrowed segment; no red-flag features
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and mild chest soreness are expected after an EGD through a narrowed segment and settle within 24-48h. Return precautions: worsening chest pain, fever, trouble breathing, vomiting blood, or black stools.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no perforation/aspiration features to escalate.

contacts.verbatim: The nurse told me to stop my baby aspirin before the test. When can I start it again, and when will I hear about the biopsy?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about aspirin resumption timing and pending pathology; aspirin was held 5 days pre-procedure
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: aspirin may resume at 48h post-biopsy if no bleeding; document on reconciled med list. Pathology expected ~day 4 (2026-07-25); team will call with the result.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the med_resume (held aspirin) item.

contacts.verbatim: There was a little pink when I spit after brushing my teeth this morning. Feeling fine otherwise, eating soft food okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace blood-tinged saliva after tooth-brushing in an otherwise well patient; no hematemesis, melena, lightheadedness, or ongoing bleeding — benign despite alarming wording
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a trace of pink after brushing is not GI bleeding. Counsel to watch for and call immediately if vomiting blood, black/tarry stools, lightheadedness, or persistent bleeding.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign triage trap: minor oral spotting is not post-biopsy hemorrhage; reinforce the true red-flag precautions without escalating.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks since my scope and nobody has ever called me about the biopsy. I'm worried — they said it might be something serious. What did it show?
contacts.clinical_signal: Dropped follow-up: patient with a mass highly suspicious for malignancy has an unread/uncommunicated pathology result nearly two weeks out; expected back ~day 4
contacts.expected_action: Escalate to GI same day: retrieve the pending esophageal-mass pathology, confirm it has resulted, and have the physician disclose the result and initiate the oncology/staging pathway; document that the loop was closed.
contacts.rationale: A near-certain esophageal malignancy result left uncommunicated for ~12 days is a dropped critical follow-up; the contact must escalate to surface and close the pending_pathology item rather than merely reassure.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and swallowing is a little sore today, but water and soft food are going down fine.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD/post-dilation sore throat and transient odynophagia; tolerating liquids and soft diet, no obstruction
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and swallowing tenderness are expected after scope + dilation and settle over 24-72h. Advise soft diet, fluids, and return precautions for inability to swallow saliva, chest pain, or fever.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-dilation symptoms with preserved swallowing; no red flag for perforation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear about the biopsy from my esophagus, and when is it safe for me to start my apixaban and baby aspirin again?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and anticoagulant/antiplatelet resumption timing
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies pending (~day 5, expected 2026-08-11); team will call with result and plan. Confirm apixaban 2.5 mg BID and aspirin 81 mg resumption timing per the cardiology-coordinated plan (typically 24-48h if no bleeding).
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending_pathology (F1) and the med_resume items (F2/F3).

contacts.verbatim: I feel a little bloated and burpy since the test, but the swallowing is already better than before — meat went down okay tonight.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy insufflation bloating; improving dysphagia consistent with successful dilation
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; bloating/gas from insufflation is self-limited, and improved solid-food swallowing reflects a good dilation result. Reinforce omeprazole adherence and return precautions.
contacts.rationale: Benign insufflation symptom with clinical improvement; no escalation warranted.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and I'm kind of burpy and bloated since the scope. Otherwise okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and air insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating are expected after upper endoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsy results? And should I keep taking my fish oil blood thinner or stop it now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Question about pending duodenal pathology plus 'blood thinner' confusion (OTC fish oil)
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: duodenal pathology pending (~day 4-5), team will call with the Marsh grade and confirm or exclude celiac. Clarify the fish oil is an OTC omega-3 supplement, not a true blood thinner — no need to stop or hold it; it has been added to the medication list.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and closes the med-confusion loop (trap: 'blood thinner' is benign fish oil).

contacts.verbatim: Since yesterday I've had a fever of about 101.5, a cough with some junk coming up, and I get short of breath just walking to the kitchen. It started the day after the scope.
contacts.clinical_signal: Fever, productive cough, and dyspnea 2 days post-sedation EGD — concern for aspiration pneumonitis/pneumonia from procedural sedation
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day evaluation (urgent clinic or ED): assess oxygen saturation, chest exam, and chest imaging for post-sedation aspiration; treat as aspiration pneumonia if confirmed. Do not manage as benign post-endoscopy symptoms.
contacts.rationale: New fever plus productive cough and exertional dyspnea shortly after sedated upper endoscopy is a genuine post-procedure complication (aspiration) requiring prompt in-person evaluation, not reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a little pink tinge when I spit the first day or two after the biopsies. It's gone now — was that a problem?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace transient blood-tinged saliva after mucosal duodenal biopsies, self-resolved
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; minor transient pink-tinged saliva after superficial mucosal biopsies is expected and self-limited. No action needed given it has resolved and there is no ongoing bleeding, black stools, or lightheadedness.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign trap: minor self-resolved spotting after mucosal biopsy is not a bleeding complication and correctly resolves to reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's sore and it feels a little tight in my chest when I swallow, like the food sticks for a second.
contacts.clinical_signal: Mild odynophagia/dysphagia and retrosternal discomfort day 1 after esophageal band ligation — expected post-banding symptom from band sites; no fever, no bleeding, no drooling/obstruction
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; transient sore throat, mild chest tightness and short-lived food-sticking are expected after band ligation. Advise soft/liquid diet, chew well, take meals slowly, sit upright, and use prescribed PPI/sucralfate. Return for high fever, severe or worsening chest pain, inability to swallow saliva, vomiting blood, or black stools.
contacts.rationale: TRIAGE TRAP (dangerous-sounding, benign): 'chest tightness on swallowing' after banding sounds alarming but is a common self-limited post-band symptom; escalate only if accompanied by red-flag features (bleeding, obstruction, fever, severe pain).

contacts.verbatim: My daughter buys me the fish oil pills she calls my blood thinner. I stopped them for the test — should I start taking them again now, and is there a new heart pill I'm supposed to begin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Medication reconciliation question — OTC omega-3 fish oil (mislabeled 'blood thinner', not a true anticoagulant, never required holding) plus query about starting the planned non-selective beta-blocker for variceal secondary prophylaxis
contacts.expected_action: Answer/reconcile: the fish oil is an OTC supplement, not a prescription blood thinner, and never needed to be held — she may resume it, and it poses no post-band bleeding concern. Confirm the carvedilol (NSBB) start/titration plan with GI/hepatology and how to take it given blood pressure/ascites.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the chart and follow-up plan; links to the beta-blocker initiation item (F3) and closes the fish-oil 'blood thinner' confusion — no true anticoagulant was ever involved.

contacts.verbatim: I threw up some blood a little while ago and my stool this morning was black and tarry again. I feel weak and dizzy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Hematemesis plus melena with orthostatic lightheadedness ~11 days post-banding — classic timing for post-band ulcer bleeding / recurrent variceal hemorrhage in a Child-Pugh B cirrhotic with high-risk varices
contacts.expected_action: Escalate immediately for emergency evaluation (call 911 / ED activation); anticipate urgent EGD for hemostasis, resuscitation, transfusion as needed, and hepatology/GI notification. Do not manage as outpatient.
contacts.rationale: Post-band ulcer bleed/rebleed with active hematemesis, melena, and a hemodynamic symptom is a life-threatening red-flag complication in a cirrhotic with known high-risk varices (F1) requiring emergent action.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is scratchy and it felt a little sore when I swallowed last night, but it's already better this morning. Is that normal after the scope?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat / mild transient odynophagia after esophageal biopsies; already improving, no fever, no trouble swallowing secretions
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and transient soreness on swallowing are expected after EGD with biopsies and typically resolve within 24-48h. Cool/soft foods and fluids as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptom that is already improving; no red-flag features (no fever, chest pain at rest, or inability to swallow saliva).

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear about my biopsy results? And should I keep taking the drugstore omeprazole or stop it now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and how to manage the self-started OTC PPI
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected back ~2026-08-01; GI will call with the diagnosis and treatment plan. Advise not to change the omeprazole on his own — the team will set a definitive PPI/therapy plan at result review since PPI exposure affects biopsy interpretation.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending pathology item and the medication-reconciliation plan (F1/F5).

contacts.verbatim: I feel back to normal. Can I go back to eating regular food, or do I still need to cut everything up small and take it slow?
contacts.clinical_signal: Diet/return-to-activity question; untreated narrow-caliber esophagus means bolus-impaction precautions still apply until EoE is treated
contacts.expected_action: Reinforce continued impaction precautions until EoE is diagnosed and treated: chew thoroughly, small bites, fluids with meals, avoid large solid boluses. Return to ED if food gets stuck or he cannot swallow his own saliva.
contacts.rationale: Benign, answerable question; reinforces the standing return/safety-netting precautions given the persistent food-impaction risk (F6).

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm kind of burpy and bloated since the camera test. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating/gas are expected after EGD and settle within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will we hear back about the biopsies you took from her stomach? Her daughter is asking.
contacts.clinical_signal: Family asking about pending gastric biopsy pathology (malignancy exclusion + H. pylori)
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: ulcer-margin and H. pylori biopsies pending, expected back ~2026-07-27; GI will call the daughter with the result and next steps once resulted.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item F1.

contacts.verbatim: I found Mom's old rivaroxaban bottle — it says 20 milligrams. Should I start giving it to her again now that the scope is done?
contacts.clinical_signal: Caregiver about to resume anticoagulation prematurely and at the stale (wrong) dose
contacts.expected_action: Counsel: do NOT resume rivaroxaban yet — it is intentionally held pending GI/cardiology coordination after the ulcer bleed. The correct dose is 15 mg daily (not 20 mg); discard/ignore the old 20 mg bottle. GI/cardiology will confirm the safe restart date and dose.
contacts.rationale: Med-timing/dose confusion trap: a benign-sounding logistics question that, if unaddressed, leads to early resumption at the wrong (higher) dose and rebleeding risk. Maps to dose-reconciliation item F5 (and the held-med resumption pathway F3).

contacts.verbatim: She threw up some dark blood this morning and her stool was black and tarry. She seems weak and a bit dizzy when she stands up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Recurrent hematemesis + melena with orthostatic symptoms — ulcer rebleed
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation; anticipate repeat EGD for hemostasis, keep rivaroxaban held, check CBC/type-and-screen, resuscitate as needed.
contacts.rationale: Delayed rebleeding from the antral ulcer with a hemodynamic symptom in an elderly patient is a genuine red-flag complication requiring emergent action; maps to rebleeding-surveillance item F7.

patients.procedure: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with gastric biopsy and H. pylori culture/susceptibility

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and I've been kind of burpy and bloated since the camera test. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and insufflation bloating/belching after a diagnostic scope with biopsies
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after EGD and settle within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges; call for fever, worsening abdominal or chest pain, black stools, or vomiting blood.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms after a diagnostic EGD; no complication, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will you know about the stomach biopsies, and what antibiotic will I get since I'm allergic to penicillin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending H. pylori culture/susceptibility timing and allergy-constrained salvage regimen
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: culture/susceptibility takes about 10-14 days (expected ~2026-08-18); GI will call with results and choose a non-amoxicillin, susceptibility-guided regimen given the penicillin allergy. Stay off antibiotics until then.
contacts.rationale: Answerable directly from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending culture/susceptibility item (F1) and the documented penicillin allergy.

contacts.verbatim: Can I start taking my pantoprazole again for the burning, or am I supposed to keep it stopped?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about PPI resumption timing after the biopsy hold
contacts.expected_action: Answer: pantoprazole may be resumed now for symptom control since the biopsies are already obtained; it will need to be held again for 2 weeks before any future test-of-cure. Use antacid/H2 as needed in the meantime.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the medication-reconciliation follow-up (F5); PPI was held only for biopsy accuracy and can safely resume post-procedure.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm burping and gassy since yesterday. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-procedure sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear about the biopsies, and could you remind me exactly when I should start my warfarin injections back up? I don't want to get that wrong with my valve.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and warfarin/LMWH bridging resumption schedule
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected ~2026-08-04, team will call with results and surveillance interval; restate the cardiology/anticoag-clinic bridging restart dates (LMWH overlap until INR 2.5-3.5) and confirm she has them in writing.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the high-stakes warfarin resumption item. Correct info prevents both valve thrombosis and premature-resumption bleeding.

contacts.verbatim: The pharmacy asked about my baby aspirin. Should I be taking it again yet or not?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient seeking guidance on held OTC aspirin resumption decision
contacts.expected_action: Advise she should not resume the OTC aspirin on her own; the resume-vs-discontinue decision is pending cardiology given additive bleeding risk on warfarin. Relay to cardiology/GI to close out and document on the med list.
contacts.rationale: Answerable and links to the aspirin reconciliation item; keeping her off aspirin until cardiology decides is the safe course, no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a small pink streak on the toilet paper once this morning. No dizziness, no more after that, and my stool looked normal. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Single small streak of blood without ongoing bleeding, hematochezia, or hemodynamic symptoms — dangerous-sounding-but-benign in context of a clean exam
contacts.expected_action: Reassure: a one-time minor pink streak with normal stool and no lightheadedness is common after polypectomy. Reinforce delayed-bleeding return precautions — call/ED for repeated or larger volume dark-red blood, clots, lightheadedness, or fainting — given she is resuming anticoagulation.
contacts.rationale: Benign minor spotting; but because she is anticoagulated with a ~14-day delayed-bleed window, the reassurance must be paired with explicit escalation triggers. No current red flag.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I'm burping and bloated from all the air. Otherwise okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation bloating; no red flags
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected and self-limited over 24-48h; walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The heart office said I could start my Brilinta back up. Is that alright after they took that big polyp, and when do I hear about the biopsy?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient confirming ticagrelor resumption timing (held 3 days pre-procedure, DES <12 months) and asking about pending EMR pathology
contacts.expected_action: Confirm cardiology-directed prompt resumption of ticagrelor is appropriate given DES <12 months; counsel that this raises delayed post-EMR bleeding risk so watch for warning signs; note pathology expected ~day 6 and the team will call with results and surveillance interval.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the antithrombotic plan and follow-up items; links to med_resume (F2) and touches pending pathology (F1).

contacts.verbatim: I've passed a good amount of dark red blood and some clots into the toilet twice this afternoon and I'm feeling dizzy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-EMR/post-polypectomy hemorrhage after 22 mm piecemeal sigmoid EMR, on continued aspirin plus resumed ticagrelor, with orthostatic/hemodynamic symptom
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation; assess hemodynamics/CBC; hold ticagrelor pending evaluation and coordinate urgently with cardiology given DES; anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis at the clipped EMR site.
contacts.rationale: Delayed post-EMR hemorrhage with lightheadedness is a genuine red-flag complication amplified by resumed antiplatelet therapy — the anticipated one_complication event for this high-risk EMR.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a little pink on the toilet paper after a bowel movement today. No dizziness, feeling fine. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor streaking on tissue consistent with known internal hemorrhoids; no volume of blood, no hemodynamic symptoms, well outside the highest-risk delayed-bleed window
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scant pink on tissue is consistent with the documented internal hemorrhoids; advise fiber/hydration and to call back or seek care if there is a larger volume of dark red blood, clots, or lightheadedness.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign triage trap — a small amount of pink on the tissue in an asymptomatic patient with known hemorrhoids should be reassured, not escalated, and contrasts with the true bleed in C3.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I've been really gassy and bloated since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatulence
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Classic benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The nurse mentioned my old blood thinner before the test. I still have that half bottle of apixaban in the cabinet — should I start taking it again now that the scope is done?
contacts.clinical_signal: Medication-timing confusion — patient is NOT anticoagulated (completed provoked-DVT course, stopped ~Jan 2026); no anticoagulant was held and none should be resumed
contacts.expected_action: Counsel clearly: do NOT restart apixaban. Her DVT course was completed and discontinued; nothing was held for the procedure, so there is nothing to resume. Reinforce stopping pharmacy auto-refills and safely disposing of leftover tablets.
contacts.rationale: Med-timing confusion trap — a wrong 'resume anticoagulation' answer would inappropriately re-anticoagulate a patient with no indication and add bleeding risk; correct disposition is to counsel against restarting.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a tiny bit of pink on the toilet paper once today. No real bleeding, I feel completely fine otherwise. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor self-limited spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of a small polyp; hemodynamically well, isolated and trivial
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a trace of pink after a small cold-snare polypectomy is expected and self-limited. Give return precautions: call or seek care for repeated dark red blood, clots, black stools, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign trap — trace spotting after low-risk cold-snare polypectomy in a non-anticoagulated patient does not warrant escalation, but return precautions must be stated.

contacts.verbatim: It's been about a week and I haven't heard anything about the biopsy from my colonoscopy. When will I find out the results?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient following up on pending polypectomy pathology and next surveillance interval
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: polypectomy pathology was expected back ~2026-08-09; GI team will call with the result and set the next surveillance colonoscopy interval. Confirm the loop is open and owned by GI.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result; links to the pending_pathology item (F1) and prompts closing the results-communication loop.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a little scratchy and I'm more tired than usual, but I got home okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and residual sedation grogginess/fatigue; baseline anemia fatigue
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and grogginess are expected and self-limited. Reinforce return precautions for heavy bleeding, dizziness, or worsening fatigue.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms; no red flag and no escalation warranted.

contacts.verbatim: My kidney doctor's blood thinner and the fish oil are still stopped — when do I start them again? I don't want a stroke.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about anticoagulation (dabigatran) and fish-oil resumption; deliberate deferred-hold situation given active bleeding source
contacts.expected_action: Explain the hold is intentional pending hemostasis and pathology; do NOT self-resume dabigatran or fish oil; GI and nephrology will jointly decide timing. Address stroke-risk concern and confirm plan.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the result and follow-up plan; links to the deferred anticoagulation-resumption item. Counsel against premature self-restart of a renally-cleared DOAC in a patient with an active bleeding lesion.

contacts.verbatim: I've gone to the bathroom with a good bit of dark red blood a couple times today and I feel woozy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed lower GI bleeding from the biopsied friable mass / post-APC site with a hemodynamic symptom in a CKD4 patient
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation; keep dabigatran and fish oil held; check hemoglobin/vitals and type-and-screen; anticipate possible repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis and transfusion per threshold.
contacts.rationale: Overt rectal bleeding with lightheadedness after biopsy/APC of a friable bleeding mass is a true red-flag complication requiring urgent action, especially with baseline anemia and CKD4.

contacts.verbatim: Nobody ever called me about the biopsy they took from that growth. It's been almost two weeks and I'm scared it's cancer — what did it show?
contacts.clinical_signal: Dropped follow-up — pending pathology on a mass suspicious for malignancy never communicated to the patient
contacts.expected_action: Retrieve the pending/resulted pathology, have GI call the patient promptly with the result and plan, and confirm the oncology/surgery referral pathway is moving; close the loop on a suspicious-for-malignancy biopsy that should already have been communicated.
contacts.rationale: Dropped-ball signal: a critical pending-pathology result on a mass suspicious for cancer was not communicated within the expected 3-5 day window; must be escalated to schedule/callback and result disclosure, not reassured away.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm really bloated and burping a lot since yesterday.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and retained-insufflation bloating/gas
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and resolve within 24-48h. Encourage walking and warm fluids.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out about the polyps you took out, and is it okay to take my ibuprofen and aspirin again for my knees?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending polyp pathology and resuming routine OTC NSAID/aspirin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-11); team will call with histology and surveillance interval. Confirm she may resume OTC ibuprofen/aspirin per comfort — she is not on anticoagulation, so no bridging or special timing applies.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item. No anticoagulant was held, so the medication question is reassurance, not a hold/resume risk.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a tiny streak of pink on the toilet paper once today. No pain and nothing since. Should I be worried?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace self-limited spotting after small cold-snare/cold-forceps polypectomy; no hemodynamic symptoms, single episode, no ongoing bleeding
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; minor trace spotting after small cold polypectomy is common and self-limited. Provide return precautions: call or seek care for repeated or large-volume red blood, clots, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: a single trace of pink after a clean exam with small cold-technique removals is expected and low risk. Reassure with clear escalation triggers rather than escalate.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and I was pretty groggy and worn out yesterday after the anesthesia.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and residual sedation grogginess
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and next-day grogginess after MAC are expected and self-limited. Push fluids, rest.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms in an 81-year-old after MAC sedation; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: Nobody has called about my stomach biopsy yet. Also — when can I go back on my edoxaban? And is my fish oil okay to keep taking?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending gastric/H. pylori pathology and about anticoagulation/fish-oil resumption timing after a high-risk ulcer
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the record: gastric H. pylori pathology still pending (expected ~2026-07-29); team will call with result. Do NOT resume edoxaban or fish oil independently — restart date/dose is being set jointly by GI and cardiology (edoxaban 30 mg, not 60 mg); hold fish oil during active ulcer.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending pathology (F3) and the anticoagulation/med-reconciliation items (F2/F8).

contacts.verbatim: I've had two black tarry bowel movements since last night and I felt dizzy and clammy when I stood up this morning.
contacts.clinical_signal: Recurrent melena with orthostatic symptoms — rebleeding from the treated Forrest IIa duodenal ulcer in an anticoagulated, anemic patient
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation / same-day admission: recurrent melena + orthostasis after high-risk ulcer hemostasis. Ensure edoxaban and fish oil held, IV access, type & crossmatch, serial CBC, high-dose IV PPI, urgent repeat EGD for re-treatment.
contacts.rationale: Recurrent melena plus a hemodynamic symptom after a Forrest IIa ulcer is high-risk rebleeding — the index critical result (F1) becoming an active emergency.

contacts.verbatim: I felt so much better that I started my edoxaban back up on my own three days ago, and my fish oil too. That's fine, right?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient self-resumed anticoagulation and OTC antiplatelet fish oil early, without the planned GI/cardiology sign-off, after a high-risk bleeding ulcer
contacts.expected_action: Escalate/counsel urgently: patient resumed edoxaban and fish oil against plan and before hemostasis-confirmed sign-off — elevated early-rebleeding risk. Contact same day, instruct to hold fish oil, confirm the coordinated GI/cardiology restart decision and correct dose (30 mg), assess for interval bleeding symptoms.
contacts.rationale: Med-timing trap: benign-sounding self-report masks premature anticoagulant/antiplatelet resumption after a high-risk ulcer — the load-bearing resumption decision (F2) done wrong.

contacts.verbatim: I came in today but they didn't do the camera test. I'm confused — was my colonoscopy cancelled?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient confusion about deferral of the elective colonoscopy
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the visit: the colonoscopy was intentionally deferred because her heart stent is only ~2 months old; it will be rescheduled (~1 year post-stent) after cardiology clears the timing.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the deferral decision and reschedule plan; links to the repeat_procedure item.

contacts.verbatim: You said not to stop my heart pills — should I just start my baby aspirin back up again today? I still have the bottle.
contacts.clinical_signal: Med-timing confusion — patient wants to unilaterally restart the self-discontinued aspirin before cardiology weighs in
contacts.expected_action: Escalate to the team/cardiology: do not have her self-restart until cardiology confirms the resumption plan; ensure the urgent cardiology message about the DAPT interruption is actioned and she receives explicit instructions.
contacts.rationale: Unsanctioned DAPT changes ~2 months post-DES carry stent-thrombosis/bleeding implications; the resumption decision belongs to cardiology, so this is not a simple reassure — a triage trap (routine-sounding med question with real risk).

contacts.verbatim: I've had some pressure in my chest on and off since yesterday and I get a little short of breath walking to the kitchen.
contacts.clinical_signal: Intermittent chest pressure and exertional dyspnea in a patient ~2 months post-DES who has interrupted her aspirin — possible stent thrombosis/ACS
contacts.expected_action: Escalate emergently — direct to emergency care / call cardiology immediately; chest pressure with dyspnea after an unsanctioned antiplatelet interruption early post-DES is a possible stent thrombosis and cannot wait.
contacts.rationale: Benign-sounding intermittent chest pressure is dangerous in this cardiac context; the deferral rationale (recent DES on interrupted DAPT) makes this an emergent escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will you actually schedule my colonoscopy, and does my heart doctor need to sign off first?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient following up on rescheduling and the required cardiology clearance
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: rescheduling targets ~1 year post-stent and yes, cardiology consultation/clearance is required first; confirm the coordination is in progress.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the reschedule and cardiology-coordination plan; links to the repeat_procedure item.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I've been pretty bloated and gassy since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and colonic insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat from the scope and gas/bloating from air insufflation are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Walking and passing gas help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the polyps you took out? And should I keep taking my baby aspirin like normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology timing and whether to continue aspirin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected back ~2026-08-08; the GI team will call with results and the surveillance interval. Confirm he should continue low-dose aspirin 81 mg without interruption — it was never held and no anticoagulant is involved.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item. Aspirin was continued throughout, so the correct guidance is simply to keep taking it — no resumption decision exists.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a tiny streak of pink on the toilet paper once after a bowel movement today. No pain, feel totally fine otherwise.
contacts.clinical_signal: Trivial self-limited spotting after cold-snare polypectomy; hemodynamically well, single minor episode, no clots or ongoing bleeding
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of pink/light spotting after cold-snare polypectomy is common and benign. Advise return precautions: call or seek care for repeated or large-volume red blood, clots, black stools, lightheadedness, dizziness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap (dangerous-sounding-but-benign): scant one-time spotting after low-risk cold-snare removal in an asymptomatic, hemodynamically stable patient is reassurable — not the delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage pattern, which involves recurrent/large red blood or hemodynamic symptoms. Return precautions given so a true bleed would be caught.

patients.procedure: Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) with sphincterotomy and stone extraction

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm still kind of groggy and worn out from the sedation, but nothing hurts.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and lingering sedation grogginess; no pain
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and post-sedation fatigue are expected and self-limited within 24-48h. Hydrate and rest.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-ERCP/sedation symptoms with no abdominal pain or red flags; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The pain in the pit of my stomach is back and it's boring straight through to my back, worse than before. I've thrown up twice and can't keep water down.
contacts.clinical_signal: Severe epigastric pain radiating to the back with persistent vomiting — post-ERCP pancreatitis in a patient with recent gallstone pancreatitis and PD instrumentation
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation; STAT lipase and abdominal exam; IV fluids and analgesia; admit for post-ERCP pancreatitis management.
contacts.rationale: Classic post-ERCP pancreatitis presentation and the specific complication this procedure's risk profile predicts; a red-flag complication requiring emergent action.

contacts.verbatim: I've been taking my baby aspirin the whole time — was I supposed to stop it after the procedure? And when can I start the fish oil back up?
contacts.clinical_signal: Antithrombotic reconciliation question — aspirin was continued through the procedure; fish oil held pre-procedure
contacts.expected_action: Reassure: aspirin was intentionally continued through the sphincterotomy and should keep being taken daily. Fish oil may be resumed after the cholecystectomy per the surgeon; no prescription anticoagulant is needed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the medication reconciliation plan; links to the med_reconciliation item; no held anticoagulant, so no dangerous early-resumption timing issue.

contacts.verbatim: Nobody has called me about the little tube they put in during the procedure or the x-ray you mentioned. Do I need to do something about that?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient flagging that the prophylactic pancreatic duct stent KUB confirmation has not been arranged — potential dropped follow-up on a retained stent

contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the polyp results? And I want to make sure — I'm back on my losartan and my little water pill like normal, right?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and confirming resumption of routine home meds (no anticoagulant was held)
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology pending (expected ~2026-08-16); team will call with result and surveillance interval. Confirm losartan and hydrochlorothiazide should continue as normal — neither was held for this procedure.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item. No anticoagulant was held, so this is routine reassurance, not a resumption-timing risk.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a little pink on the toilet paper after I went this morning, just the one time. No dizziness, no belly pain, I feel fine otherwise — but after a scope with those polyps taken out it scared me.
contacts.clinical_signal: TRAP (dangerous-sounding-but-benign): scant self-limited pink streak after cold-snare removal of two small (4-5 mm) polyps, no recurrence, no hemodynamic or pain symptoms — expected minor post-polypectomy spotting, not delayed hemorrhage
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a single episode of minor pink spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of small polyps is expected and self-limited. Give return precautions: call back or seek care for repeated/large-volume dark red or clotted blood, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Cold-snare removal of two diminutive polyps carries very low delayed-bleeding risk; a single scant pink streak without recurrence or systemic symptoms is benign. The trap is that any bleeding after polypectomy sounds alarming, but this presentation does not meet escalation criteria — over-triage would be the error.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and I was pretty foggy and worn out the rest of yesterday after the sedation. Feeling more like myself today.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and transient post-anesthesia grogginess, already improving; no respiratory red flag
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and lingering grogginess after MAC/propofol are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Advise return precautions if breathing worsens below baseline or fever develops.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms, already resolving; prior sedation intolerance was anticipated and monitored, no active concern.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsies from that spot they found? And when am I supposed to start my blood thinner up again — nobody's told me a day yet.

contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected ~2026-08-08; GI will personally call with the result and next steps. For apixaban, confirm the cardiology-directed resumption timing (~48-72h post-biopsy if no bleeding) and reconcile the true home dose (chart 5 mg BID vs self-reduced once daily) per F4.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending_pathology (F1) and to the anticoagulation resumption/reconciliation task (F4).

contacts.verbatim: There was a little pink tinge in my spit once when I brushed my teeth this morning. Nothing since, no real bleeding, and I feel okay otherwise.

contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a one-time trace of blood-tinged saliva after multiple esophageal biopsies is expected and benign. Reinforce bleeding red flags (repeated vomiting of blood, black/tarry stools, lightheadedness) and to call/seek care if any occur, especially once apixaban is resumed.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding but benign: minimal, single, self-resolved spotting is within normal post-biopsy expectation; links to the post-biopsy bleeding watch (F7) as counseling, not escalation. No hemodynamic or ongoing-bleeding signal present.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and I'm really gassy and bloated since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and colonic insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: I take two blood thinners — the little aspirin and the twice-a-day apixaban. I kept taking the aspirin. When exactly do I start the apixaban again? I don't want to miss a dose and have a stroke.

contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: aspirin was intentionally continued; apixaban restart date must be set jointly by GI and cardiology/EP given clip placement and high stroke risk. Provide the written restart date and confirm she has it; do not have her restart on her own until instructed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable/coordination item that maps to the critical med_resume follow-up (F2); no clinical red flag but must not be dropped.

contacts.verbatim: I've passed a bunch of dark red blood into the toilet three times this evening and there are clots. I feel dizzy and clammy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage with hemodynamic symptoms; higher risk given resected pedunculated stalk, continued aspirin, and pending apixaban restart timing
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation; do NOT restart apixaban; alert GI on-call and anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis; notify cardiology given anticoagulation/EF context.
contacts.rationale: Hematochezia with clots plus orthostatic lightheadedness is delayed post-polypectomy bleeding with a hemodynamic signal — a true complication requiring emergent action; links to complication_watch (F4).

contacts.verbatim: Nobody has called me yet about the results of the polyps you took out. Should I have heard by now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient prompting on outstanding pathology communication
contacts.expected_action: Confirm pathology status (expected back 2026-08-11); ensure a GI team member calls with the result and the surveillance interval, and document the loop is closed.
contacts.rationale: Benign informational request that maps to the pending_pathology item (F1); ensures the result is not lost to follow-up.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's kind of raw and I feel really gassy and bloated. Is that normal after all this?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-intubation/EGD sore throat plus colonoscopy insufflation bloating; no bleeding or airway symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat (intubation/scope) and gas/bloating (insufflation) are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Warm fluids, walking. Review return precautions for vomiting blood, black/tarry stools, dizziness.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy/anesthesia symptoms; no red flag despite the recent ulcer, since no bleeding or hemodynamic signal.

contacts.verbatim: Mom's a little groggy today and says her throat feels scratchy, but she ate some breakfast at the home.

contacts.expected_action: Reassure; grogginess and sore throat are expected after sedation/scope and self-limited within 24-48h. Ensure hydration; call for fever, severe abdominal pain, or bleeding.

contacts.verbatim: The assisted living nurse is asking when we should give Mom her apixaban again, and when we'll hear back about the biopsy from the growth you found.

contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of pink/streaky blood once after a cold-snare polypectomy is common and self-limited. Give return precautions: call or seek care for repeated or large-volume bleeding, clots, dizziness, black stools, or lightheadedness — especially once apixaban is resumed.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: minor one-time spotting after a small cold-snare polypectomy in a stable patient does not warrant escalation, but return precautions matter given resumed anticoagulation.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and nobody has called us about Mom's biopsy from the growth. Should we be worried? What happens next?
contacts.clinical_signal: POA following up on the pending mass pathology (expected back ~day 5); results should now be available and communicated
contacts.expected_action: Retrieve the specimen A pathology (expected back 2026-08-11) and schedule a call/visit with the POA to convey the result and next steps (staging, colorectal surgery/oncology review framed within goals of care). Ensure the result was not dropped.
contacts.rationale: Loop-closure on the critical pending pathology; links to F2. Not a clinical emergency, but flags a possible dropped follow-up on a malignancy-suspicious result that must be actively closed.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I'm burping up a lot of air since the scope. Nothing terrible.

contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after upper endoscopy and self-limited within 24-48h. Return precautions if fever, chest pain, or trouble swallowing develop.
