contacts.rationale: Answerable from the PPI reconciliation plan; links to the med_reconciliation item (F5). Prevents an unsafe duplication.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a bit scratchy from the breathing tube and my upper belly feels sore where they worked. Nothing bad, just achy.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-intubation sore throat and mild epigastric soreness at the ESD resection site; no bleeding or systemic signs
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat from intubation and mild epigastric discomfort after ESD are expected and self-limited. Advise soft diet, prescribed PPI, and to call for melena, hematemesis, severe/worsening pain, or fever.
contacts.rationale: Benign expected symptoms after ESD under general anesthesia; no red-flag features.

contacts.verbatim: My knee was killing me so I went back on my ibuprofen yesterday, and I restarted the baby aspirin too since I felt fine. Is that okay?
contacts.clinical_signal: TRIAGE TRAP (med-timing confusion): patient self-resumed NSAID and antiplatelet on day 2 after a high-bleed-risk gastric ESD, before hemostasis window per protocol — raises delayed post-ESD bleeding risk
contacts.expected_action: Escalate to GI same day: instruct patient to STOP the ibuprofen and aspirin immediately, reinforce they must remain held until hemostasis is confirmed per post-ESD protocol, counsel on delayed-bleeding warning signs, and arrange an NSAID alternative (acetaminophen) for knee pain with PCP.
contacts.rationale: Early self-resumption of an NSAID plus an antiplatelet after high-bleed-risk ESD is a live bleeding hazard; benign-sounding question but requires prompt clinical counseling, so it escalates.

contacts.verbatim: I've been sticking to soft foods. When can I eat normally again and go back to my usual meals?
contacts.clinical_signal: Routine diet-advancement question; no symptoms of bleeding or perforation reported
contacts.expected_action: Answer from discharge instructions: advance diet gradually per post-ESD protocol while continuing high-dose PPI; return precautions for melena, hematemesis, or severe pain reviewed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable informational question with no clinical red flags.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and no one ever called me about the results from the tissue they removed. I'm anxious about whether it was cancer. Did something get lost?
contacts.clinical_signal: DROPPED BALL: pending ESD pathology (curative vs non-curative margins/depth) never communicated to the patient within the expected window
contacts.expected_action: Close the loop: retrieve the ESD margin/depth pathology, have GI call the patient with the result, and schedule follow-up to review curative status and surveillance interval; if non-curative, arrange multidisciplinary/surgical referral.
contacts.rationale: Flags a dropped pathology follow-up on the critical pending_pathology item (F1); the result must be located and communicated, and follow-up scheduled.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's pretty sore and it stings a little when I swallow, but I'm still getting my liquids and soft stuff down okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-dilation sore throat / mild transient odynophagia; tolerating secretions and soft/liquid diet
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and odynophagia are expected after esophageal dilation and settle over 24-72h. Confirm she is tolerating secretions and diet with no chest/back pain, fever, or vomiting blood; reinforce return precautions.
contacts.rationale: Benign expected post-dilation symptom in a patient who is tolerating secretions and diet with no red-flag features; maps to the complication_watch item as the reassuring, non-escalation side.

contacts.verbatim: When will I find out about the biopsy you took from my throat area? And was it alright that I kept taking my fish oil like you said?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending surveillance pathology and confirming fish-oil (supplement) guidance
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: surveillance biopsy pathology expected back ~2026-08-01; the team will call with the result and document that recurrent malignancy was excluded. Confirm OTC fish oil is not a true blood thinner and required no hold — continuing it was fine.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and closes the D1 fish-oil misconception.

contacts.verbatim: Food is going down a lot better now, thank you. When should I come in for the next stretching appointment?
contacts.clinical_signal: Symptomatic improvement post-dilation; asking about scheduling next serial dilation
contacts.expected_action: Reassure that improved swallowing is the expected result; advise that dilation #5 will be scheduled in ~8-10 weeks (interval shortened given earlier restenosis) and to call sooner if solid-food sticking returns.
contacts.rationale: Answerable scheduling question mapping to the repeat_procedure item; no clinical concern.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a little scratchy and I feel groggy and worn out, and the tube site is a bit tender. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat, residual sedation grogginess, and mild peristomal tenderness the day after fresh PEG placement
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat, grogginess, and site tenderness are expected in the first 24-48h. Review warning signs (spreading redness, fever, severe pain, leaking).
contacts.rationale: Common benign early post-PEG symptoms; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: How do we give his ALS pills through the new tube now, and should he still go to the infusion center for the Radicava?
contacts.clinical_signal: Caregiver asking about enteral medication route and continuation/reconciliation of the chart-omitted edaravone infusions
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: pharmacy/neurology to confirm enteral riluzole formulation via PEG and continue scheduled edaravone (Radicava) infusions; ensure med list is updated.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the medication-reconciliation follow-up item; links to F4.

contacts.verbatim: Since last night he's breathing harder, coughing more, and he's got a fever of 101. He seems more tired than usual.
contacts.clinical_signal: New fever, worsening cough and dyspnea in an ALS patient with FVC 43% and recent microaspiration — possible aspiration pneumonia with limited respiratory reserve
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/urgent evaluation; obtain chest imaging, assess oxygenation, initiate NIV support and treat for aspiration pneumonia; coordinate with pulmonology.
contacts.rationale: Fever plus worsening respiratory symptoms in a patient with severely reduced reserve is a true complication requiring emergent action; maps to the aspiration surveillance item F1.

contacts.verbatim: A little clear fluid weeps around the tube and the skin right at the opening looks slightly pink. Should we panic?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor peristomal moisture and slight pink skin at a one-week-old stoma without fever, spreading erythema, purulence, or severe pain
contacts.expected_action: Reassure and coach site care (keep clean/dry, confirm external bolster ~3 cm); educate on true warning signs — spreading redness, warmth, pus, foul drainage, fever, or severe abdominal pain — and to call if any appear.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign triage trap: small clear leakage and faint pink skin at a fresh stoma are common and self-limited, distinct from spreading infection/peritonitis; links to the PEG site watch F2.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a little scratchy and I feel gassy and crampy after all that air. Otherwise I'm just tired from being in the hospital.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/cramping; no bleeding, no hemodynamic symptom
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat and gas/cramping are expected and self-limited within 24-48h. Encourage walking and hydration.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no bleeding or instability; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The doctors stopped my blood thinner before the procedure. When am I supposed to start the rivaroxaban again? I'm nervous about my heart and a stroke.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about anticoagulation resumption timing after held rivaroxaban; no symptoms reported
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: rivaroxaban was intentionally held; do NOT restart on your own. GI and cardiology will set and document the resumption date balancing stroke risk against rebleed risk. Confirm she has that guidance in writing.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; the correct message is to await the joint cardiology/GI resumption decision and not self-resume. Links to the med_resume item.

contacts.verbatim: I got so worried about my heart that I started taking my rivaroxaban again on my own three days ago. Now I'm seeing bright red blood in the toilet again and I felt woozy standing up this morning.
contacts.clinical_signal: Self-resumed anticoagulation too early after diverticular hemoclip hemostasis, now recurrent hematochezia with orthostatic symptom — likely rebleed unmasked by premature DOAC resumption
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation: hold rivaroxaban immediately, IV access, serial Hgb, type-and-screen, hemodynamic monitoring; anticipate repeat colonoscopy or IR angiography for rebleeding. Loop in cardiology re: anticoagulation.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap — a med-timing error (patient restarted anticoagulation before the sanctioned date) plus recurrent hematochezia and orthostasis is a red-flag rebleed on a DOAC requiring emergent action, not reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm burping a lot with some bloating. Is that normal after the camera?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/belching
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating/gas are expected after upper endoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Warm saline gargles, light diet.
contacts.rationale: Classic benign post-EGD symptoms with no red-flag features; self-care only.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsies, and did I restart the apixaban at the right time? I took it the evening after the scope.
contacts.clinical_signal: Question about pending Barrett's pathology and confirming appropriate apixaban resumption timing
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: Seattle-protocol biopsies pending (expected ~day 4-5, 2026-08-03), GI will call with the result and surveillance interval. Confirm apixaban restart the evening after a bleed-free low-risk EGD is per plan (F2) — timing was correct.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and confirms the med_resume timing was appropriate (no escalation needed).

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a tiny bit of pink when I brushed my teeth this morning. No throat pain otherwise and I feel fine. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace blood-tinged saliva after biopsy, no dysphagia/odynophagia, no hematemesis, hemodynamically well — benign minor oropharyngeal/biopsy-site trace, not GI bleeding
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a trace of pink in saliva a few days after Seattle-protocol biopsies is expected and self-limited. Return precautions: vomiting blood, black/tarry stools, chest pain, or trouble swallowing — call/seek care immediately.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: minor trace pink after biopsy in an otherwise-well patient does not indicate significant bleeding; reassure with clear return precautions rather than escalate.

contacts.verbatim: Bit of cramping and a lot of gas today, and my backside feels a little tender. Is that normal after having the polyp taken out?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EMR insufflation cramping/bloating and minor anorectal tenderness on Day 1; no bleeding, no orthostatic symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; cramping, gas, and mild tenderness are expected and self-limited. Reinforce delayed-bleeding return precautions (black/tarry stools, rectal bleeding, lightheadedness, belly pain).
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms after insufflation; no red-flag features present.

contacts.verbatim: The sheet says to start my apixaban back today, Day 2. Am I really supposed to restart the blood thinner already, and when will I hear about the biopsy?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient confirming anticoagulation resumption timing and asking about pending pathology — answerable from the plan
contacts.expected_action: Confirm apixaban 5 mg BID resumes Day 2 (2026-07-16) per the anticoagulation plan since no bleeding; re-counsel on delayed-bleeding warning signs given resumed DOAC. Advise pathology and surveillance interval will be communicated by the GI team within the week.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the med_resume item and reinforces bleeding precautions on a re-anticoagulated patient.

contacts.verbatim: Since this morning I've had two really dark, almost black tarry stools, and every time I stand up the room tips and I get dizzy. I've been back on the apixaban since Thursday like they told me.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage — melena x2 plus orthostatic lightheadedness on Day 4 after 22 mm piecemeal EMR, on apixaban resumed Day 2. Classic delayed-bleed window with a hemodynamic symptom.
contacts.expected_action: Escalate to the ED now for resuscitation, CBC/type & screen, and hemodynamic monitoring; hold apixaban (no further doses until cleared) and discuss reversal per ED; notify the endoscopist and anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis.
contacts.rationale: Melena with orthostatic vital signs on a resumed DOAC after a large EMR is a red-flag delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage requiring emergent action, not a reassurance contact.

patients.procedure: ERCP with biliary sphincterotomy and stone extraction

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm still kind of groggy from the sedation, but I made it home okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and residual sedation grogginess on the evening of the procedure
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and lingering grogginess are expected after duodenoscopy under sedation and resolve within 24h. Advise rest, hydration, no driving.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: Since around midnight I've got this bad pain up in the pit of my stomach that bores straight through to my back, and I threw up once. The pain pills barely touch it.
contacts.clinical_signal: New epigastric pain radiating to back with emesis ~8h after biliary sphincterotomy — classic post-ERCP pancreatitis pattern
contacts.expected_action: Escalate same-day: order/review STAT lipase + CBC, clinician callback within the hour, arrange in-person evaluation for IV crystalloid resuscitation and admission consideration; hold further oxycodone to avoid masking symptoms.
contacts.rationale: New pain radiating to the back plus vomiting the day after sphincterotomy is the hallmark of post-ERCP pancreatitis — a true complication requiring urgent evaluation, not reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: I'm feeling a lot better now — when can I go back to taking the pain tablets if I need them, and when do I see the surgeon about my gallbladder?
contacts.clinical_signal: Recovering patient asking about resuming analgesia and timing of cholecystectomy referral
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: resume oxycodone PRN only as needed now that the pancreatitis workup is resolved and per clinician instruction; surgery (cholecystectomy) referral to be placed after recovery from the acute event — team will contact with an appointment.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; links to the analgesia reconciliation item and reflects benign convalescent questions.

patients.procedure: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with balloon dilation of esophageal stricture

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and raw when I swallow, is that normal after the stretching?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD/dilation sore throat; tolerating swallowing, no red-flag features
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat is expected for a few days after dilation. Acetaminophen and soft/cool foods as needed; self-limited.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy/post-dilation symptom with no dysphagia to liquids, chest pain, or fever; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear about my biopsy results, and do I keep taking the pantoprazole twice a day?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and post-procedure PPI/med plan
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies preliminary benign (reflux changes, no dysplasia/malignancy), final expected ~2026-07-21 and team will document/communicate. Confirm pantoprazole 40 mg twice daily continues to prevent stricture recurrence.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and med reconciliation.

contacts.verbatim: Swallowing is a lot better and food isn't catching anymore. Can I start eating normal food again yet?
contacts.clinical_signal: Improving dysphagia with good functional result; diet-advancement question, no alarm features
contacts.expected_action: Answer: advance from soft to regular textures over the next several days as tolerated, chewing thoroughly. Reinforce return precautions (fever, chest pain, inability to swallow liquids, hematemesis, melena).
contacts.rationale: Expected favorable post-dilation course; a routine diet-advancement question answerable from the disposition instructions, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: I'm pretty gassy and bloated today and my belly feels crampy off and on. No blood or anything.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy insufflation bloating and mild cramping, no bleeding
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; gas, bloating, and mild crampy discomfort are expected after insufflation and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and passing gas help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red flags; self-care.

contacts.verbatim: The nurse told me to stop my blood thinner before the scope — my fish oil. Am I okay to start taking it again now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking when to restart his 'blood thinner,' which is OTC fish oil, not a prescription anticoagulant
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: the fish oil is an over-the-counter omega-3 supplement, not a prescription anticoagulant; it carried no bleeding-risk hold and may be restarted anytime. No anticoagulation action needed.
contacts.rationale: Terminology-trap question answerable from the record; links to the med_reconciliation item. Do not infer a missing prescription anticoagulant.

contacts.verbatim: I just passed a bowel movement with a fair amount of fresh red blood and a couple of small clots. It's the second time today. I feel okay but a little worried.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding — recurrent hematochezia with clots on day 4 after cold snare polypectomy
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day clinical evaluation; assess volume, vitals, and hemodynamics, check CBC; advise urgent care/ED if bleeding continues, increases, or lightheadedness/syncope develops. Anticipate possible repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis. Note patient is not anticoagulated (fish oil only), so no anticoagulant to hold.
contacts.rationale: Recurrent hematochezia with clots is delayed post-polypectomy bleeding — a genuine complication of this procedure requiring urgent evaluation even though the patient is currently hemodynamically stable and not on a true anticoagulant.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and I still haven't heard anything about the biopsy results or when my next colonoscopy should be. Can someone let me know?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient chasing pending pathology result and surveillance interval that have not yet been communicated
contacts.expected_action: Retrieve final polyp pathology (expected back ~2026-07-21), call the patient with the result, and document/communicate the surveillance colonoscopy interval. Close the pending-pathology loop.
contacts.rationale: Answerable/actionable from the result; flags a follow-up that should already have been closed. Links to the pending_pathology item.

contacts.verbatim: I've got some cramping and a bit of gas since the camera test, about the same as my usual colitis. Nothing worse.
contacts.clinical_signal: Mild post-sigmoidoscopy cramping/insufflation gas, not exceeding baseline flare symptoms; no new red flag
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild cramping and gas after an unsedated limited sigmoidoscopy are expected and self-limited. Advise to report fever, severe/worsening abdominal pain, abdominal distension, or a large increase in bleeding.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms at or below the patient's ongoing flare baseline; no escalation while she is monitored inpatient.

contacts.verbatim: When will you know about the biopsy results, the one testing for that virus? I keep hearing it might change my treatment.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending CMV immunostain/PCR and histology results
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: CMV immunostains/PCR and histology are pending (expected ~2026-07-22); the IBD team will review and, if CMV positive, start antiviral therapy. Reassure results are being tracked.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology CMV item (F1).

contacts.verbatim: I want to make sure I've got my medicines right — should I still be taking the prednisone and my mesalamine, and is the fish oil okay?
contacts.clinical_signal: Medication reconciliation question — steroid continuation, mesalamine adherence, OTC fish oil clarification
contacts.expected_action: Confirm per reconciled list: continue prednisone/steroid course as directed by the IBD team, continue mesalamine without gaps (adherence gap preceded the flare), and reassure that OTC fish oil is not a true anticoagulant and does not need to be stopped.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the medication-reconciliation follow-up item (F5); reinforces adherence, no clinical escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: I have some gas cramps and I saw a tiny bit of pink one time when I wiped.
contacts.clinical_signal: Mild insufflation cramping plus trace spotting after cold-snare polypectomy — expected, self-limited (dangerous-sounding-but-benign)
contacts.expected_action: Reassure via interpreter; trace pink spotting and gas cramps are expected after cold-snare polypectomy. Reinforce return precautions for larger-volume or repeated red blood, black stools, lightheadedness, or new abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: A one-time trace of pink after a clean cold-snare polypectomy is benign; only escalate for higher-volume or recurrent bleeding or hemodynamic symptoms.

contacts.verbatim: I felt fine so I already started my clopidogrel and my baby aspirin again the morning after. Was that too soon?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient resumed dual antiplatelet therapy ~24h post-polypectomy, earlier than the planned 48-72h; clip placed at ascending site — elevated delayed post-polypectomy bleeding risk (med-timing trap)
contacts.expected_action: Prompt nurse triage callback via certified Cantonese interpreter: the plan was to hold DAPT 48-72h; counsel on early-resumption bleeding risk, reinforce delayed post-polypectomy bleeding precautions and strict return criteria, and document guidance from GI on how to proceed.
contacts.rationale: Restarting clopidogrel plus aspirin ~24h after polypectomy with a clip raises delayed-bleed risk and needs prompt counseling, but with no active bleeding it is urgent counseling rather than an emergency.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks since my colonoscopy and nobody has called me about the biopsy from the polyps.
contacts.clinical_signal: Pending pathology result not communicated — dropped follow-up on the polypectomy pathology loop
contacts.expected_action: Schedule a result-communication call via certified Cantonese interpreter, close the pending pathology loop for specimens A and B, and set/communicate the surveillance interval.
contacts.rationale: Pathology was due back ~day 4 but was never communicated; this is a dropped pending_pathology follow-up (F1) that must be actively closed, and the callback must use a certified interpreter (F5).

patients.procedure: ERCP with sphincterotomy and stone extraction (fluoro-minimization protocol in pregnancy)

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy from the breathing tube and my belly is a little sore and bloated, but nothing severe. No fever and the baby's been moving fine.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-intubation sore throat plus mild post-ERCP abdominal soreness/air insufflation; no pancreatitis or bleeding features, reassuring fetal movement
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and mild bloating/soreness are expected and self-limited over 24-72h. Reinforce return precautions for severe/worsening epigastric or back pain, persistent vomiting, fever/chills, black or bloody stools, or decreased fetal movement.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-ERCP/post-intubation symptoms without red-flag features; no escalation, but return precautions matter given post-sphincterotomy pancreatitis/bleeding risk.

contacts.verbatim: I take my baby aspirin (81 mg) that my pregnancy doctor started for my blood pressure. Should I keep taking it after the procedure, or was I supposed to stop it?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about continuation of low-dose aspirin after ERCP with sphincterotomy — the aspirin was continued through the procedure, not held
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: low-dose aspirin 81 mg was intentionally continued through the sphincterotomy and should be continued for preeclampsia prophylaxis through 36 weeks per MFM. Confirm it is now reconciled onto the active med list. No resumption action is needed because it was never held.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure record; maps to the med_reconciliation/continuation item (F5) — no hold occurred, so this is reassurance/education, not a resumption instruction.

contacts.verbatim: They told me there's a stent in my bile duct and that I'll need my gallbladder out at some point. When does the stent come out, and when will the surgery be?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about indwelling biliary stent removal/exchange and timing of interval cholecystectomy
contacts.expected_action: Answer and schedule: the 7 Fr plastic biliary stent requires a planned removal/exchange procedure (repeat ERCP/endoscopy) so it does not occlude — ensure this is on the books and tracked. Explain interval laparoscopic cholecystectomy timing is coordinated with MFM/surgery (2nd trimester vs postpartum). Confirm both are entered as pending follow-ups.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; links to the stent removal repeat_procedure item (F1) and touches the cholecystectomy referral (F4). Ensures the retained stent is not lost to follow-up in a pregnant patient.

patients.procedure: Colonoscopy with ileal intubation and biopsies

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I'm super gassy and bloated. Also saw a tiny bit of blood when I wiped — is that from the biopsies?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat + insufflation bloating; minor spotting after multiple segmental biopsies with no volume or hemodynamic concern
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat, gas/bloating, and trace spotting after biopsies are expected and self-limited. Advise return precautions for heavy bleeding, fever, or severe pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: a little blood after multiple biopsies with no volume, lightheadedness, or ongoing bleeding is expected — reassure with return precautions.

contacts.verbatim: Since last night my lower belly on the left has been really hurting, worse when I move, and I feel hot and shivery — I think I have a fever. It hurts to press on my stomach.
contacts.clinical_signal: New severe localized abdominal pain with peritoneal features and fever 1-2 days post-colonoscopy with deep-ulcer biopsies — concern for perforation/microperforation or localized peritonitis
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate evaluation / ED referral; NPO, urgent exam, CBC and abdominal imaging (CT) to assess for perforation; involve GI and surgery.
contacts.rationale: True complication contact: worsening localized abdominal pain with peritoneal signs and fever after biopsies of deeply ulcerated bowel is a red-flag post-procedure complication requiring emergent action.

contacts.verbatim: When am I going to hear about my biopsy results, and when do I get that belly MRI scheduled?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and the outstanding MR enterography
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected back ~2026-07-28, IBD team will call with histology and next steps; confirm MR enterography is being scheduled within 1-2 weeks.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item.

contacts.verbatim: My cramps are acting up again — can I just take my ibuprofen for it, or should I stick with Tylenol?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient reconsidering NSAID use after being counseled to stop; NSAIDs can worsen IBD inflammation
contacts.expected_action: Counsel to avoid ibuprofen/NSAIDs (may worsen intestinal inflammation); use acetaminophen; if pain uncontrolled, contact GI rather than resuming NSAIDs.
contacts.rationale: Med-timing/med-choice question that maps to the NSAID-cessation reconciliation item; reinforce the counseling rather than allow NSAID resumption.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm pretty bloated and passing a lot of gas today.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatulence
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating from air insufflation are expected and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red flags; self-care guidance only.

contacts.verbatim: When I wiped after a bowel movement this morning there was a little pink on the toilet paper. Should I be worried? I feel completely fine otherwise, no pain, no dizziness.
contacts.clinical_signal: TRAP (dangerous-sounding, benign): minor scant spotting after diminutive cold-snare polypectomy in a patient on NO anticoagulant, with no volume of blood, no clots, no pain, and no hemodynamic symptoms — expected minor self-limited oozing, not delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage
contacts.expected_action: Reassure: a small amount of pink/spotting after diminutive cold-snare polypectomy is common and self-limited, especially off anticoagulation. Provide return precautions — call back or seek care for repeated passage of a moderate/large amount of red blood or clots, ongoing bleeding, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Scant spotting with no bleeding volume, pain, or hemodynamic symptoms after tiny cold-snare polypectomy off anticoagulation is benign; the trap is that any post-polypectomy 'blood' reads as urgent but this presentation is reassure-with-precautions, not escalate.

contacts.verbatim: It's been over a week — when will I find out what the biopsies showed, and does my dad's colon cancer change how often I need this done?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology results and how the newly reported family history affects her surveillance interval
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: two diminutive polyps removed, pathology expected back ~2026-08-11; the team will call with results and set the surveillance interval, which will also factor in her father's colon cancer (first-degree family history raises risk above average and may shorten the interval).
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item (F1) and the surveillance/family-history items (F2, F3).

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a little scratchy and I have some mild burning in my upper belly, but nothing bad.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat plus mild residual dyspepsia; no alarm features
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and mild upper-abdominal burning are expected and self-limited. Continue PPI; return precautions reinforced.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms in a treated-ulcer patient; no red flag, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsy from my stomach? And can I start taking my naproxen again for my back now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Asking about pending pathology and, importantly, about resuming the NSAID that is the likely ulcer culprit
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies pending (~2026-07-27), team will call with result. Counsel that naproxen must NOT be resumed — it likely caused the ulcer; coordinate alternative analgesia (e.g., acetaminophen) with PCP.
contacts.rationale: Benign but a med-timing trap — the patient wants to restart the culprit NSAID. Answerable/counsel from the plan; links to the med_reconciliation NSAID-avoidance item.

contacts.verbatim: My stools have been normal brown and I feel back to myself. Do I really still need another scope in a couple months?
contacts.clinical_signal: No rebleeding (normal brown stools, feels well); asking about the planned surveillance EGD
contacts.expected_action: Reassure that resolution of dark stools is a good sign; explain the repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks is still required to confirm gastric ulcer healing and review pending malignancy-exclusion biopsy. Schedule the surveillance scope.
contacts.rationale: Benign course with an answerable question; links to the repeat_procedure surveillance item. No escalation.

patients.procedure: Endoscopic ultrasound with fine-needle aspiration (EUS-FNA) of mediastinal station 7 lymph node

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a bit scratchy and sore when I swallow, but I'm eating okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected mild post-transesophageal-EUS sore throat; tolerating oral intake
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat after upper endoscopy/EUS is expected and self-limited over 24-48h; lozenges/warm fluids.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptom with preserved swallowing and intake; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear what the sample showed, and when should I start my belly injection again?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending cytology result and resumption of prophylactic enoxaparin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: final cytology expected ~2026-07-28, team will call with result and route to oncology; confirm the daily prophylactic enoxaparin 40 mg can resume that evening/next day if no bleeding (no bridging needed).
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending_pathology (F1) and the med_resume item (F4).

contacts.verbatim: Since yesterday I've had a fever with chills and a deep aching pain in my chest and upper back, and it hurts more when I swallow.
contacts.clinical_signal: Fever/chills with retrosternal/back pain and odynophagia several days after mediastinal EUS-FNA — concern for mediastinitis / esophageal micro-perforation / infected node
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation: exam, labs, and CT chest to assess for mediastinitis/perforation; do not treat as benign post-procedure soreness; start broad-spectrum antibiotics per surgical/thoracic input if confirmed.
contacts.rationale: Fever plus new chest/back pain and worsening odynophagia is the classic presentation of mediastinitis or esophageal perforation after transesophageal mediastinal FNA — a true, time-critical complication.

contacts.verbatim: The scan area on my chest still feels a little tender when I press on it — is that something to worry about?
contacts.clinical_signal: Localized mild chest-wall tenderness, no fever/dyspnea/systemic signs reported
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; isolated mild tenderness without fever, worsening pain, dyspnea, or swallowing difficulty is expected residual soreness; provide return precautions (fever, worsening chest pain, trouble breathing/swallowing).
contacts.rationale: Benign residual soreness with no systemic red-flag features; safe to reassure with clear return precautions.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I've been really gassy and bloated since I got home. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-procedure sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/flatulence after diagnostic colonoscopy
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild throat irritation and gas/bloating from air insufflation are expected and self-limited over 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help. Return precautions for severe or worsening abdominal pain, fever, or bleeding.
contacts.rationale: Classic benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no red flag, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor took out a couple of little polyps — when will I find out what they were, and how often do I need to come back given my mom and brother?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending polyp pathology and shortened surveillance interval driven by family history
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: two diminutive polyps removed, pathology expected back ~2026-08-09; team will call with results and confirm the shortened surveillance interval (planned ~5 years, adjusted by pathology/genetics). Reinforce the genetics referral offer.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item (F1) and touches surveillance/genetics planning.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a tiny bit of pink on the toilet paper once yesterday. No real blood, just a little tinge. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace/minor spotting after diminutive cold-snare and cold-forceps polypectomy in a patient on no anticoagulation — within the benign expected range, no hemodynamic symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a single episode of trace pink spotting after diminutive polypectomy (cold technique, no anticoagulant) is expected and self-limited. Give clear return precautions: escalate immediately for repeated or larger volumes of red/maroon blood, clots, dizziness, or abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: minor spotting after cold polypectomy without anticoagulation is low risk; disposition is reassurance with explicit escalation criteria rather than escalation.
